classification.spec_type_rationale: RequestBar is one new organism bolted onto 5 accepted specs (001/002/003/004/005) + 006 placement rule; the real risk is integration correctness, not net-new scaffolding.

spec_seeds.overview: Add a RequestBar organism for the active request tab: a color-coded HTTP-method dropdown (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD), a plain URL text input bound to the active tab's RequestSpec.url, and Send/Save/Share actions with ⌘↵ (Send) and ⌘S (Save) shortcuts. The feature reuses the existing 001 Dropdown molecule + Icon atom, the 005 .method/[data-mstyle] color convention, and the 004 tabsStore/RequestSpec model — its only net-new primitive is a generic tabsStore spec-edit action (updateActiveSpec) and a single-source lib/httpMethods.ts. HTTP execution, the response, and {{variable}} highlighting (epic D) are explicitly out of scope.

acceptance_criteria.statement: The renderer shall provide a RequestBar organism module with a sibling token-bound stylesheet under the organisms directory.
The renderer shall provide an httpMethods module under the lib directory exporting the HTTP-method list and the HttpMethod type.
The build shall reuse the existing Radix and zustand dependencies without adding a new overlay or state-management library.
WHILE the TabBar is rendered, the tabsStore shall expose its existing lifecycle actions (openFromCollection, newBlank, close, selectActive, markClean) with unchanged behavior.
WHEN requestSpec.method is narrowed from string to the HttpMethod union, the makeBlankRequest GET seed and all existing spec assignments shall remain valid and continue to type-check.
The renderer shall keep Shell as the sole writer of the document data-mstyle attribute.
WHEN the user selects a method from the RequestBar method dropdown, the RequestBar shall write that method to the active tab's RequestSpec through updateActiveSpec.
WHEN the user edits the RequestBar URL input, the RequestBar shall write the new value to the active tab's RequestSpec url through updateActiveSpec.
WHEN updateActiveSpec receives a patch that changes at least one value, the tabsStore shall shallow-merge the patch into the active tab's spec and set that tab dirty.
IF updateActiveSpec receives a patch whose values equal the active tab's current spec, THEN the tabsStore shall not set the dirty flag.
WHEN the active tab changes, the RequestBar shall render the newly active tab's method and url and shall not leak the previous tab's values.
WHILE the URL is empty after trimming, the RequestBar shall render Send disabled and shall not emit a send intent.

WHEN Send is activated with a URL that is non-empty after trimming, the RequestBar shall invoke onSend with the active tab identity and the current method and url, and shall not perform any HTTP request.
WHILE the active tab is dirty and the user activates Save, the RequestBar shall call markClean for the active tab.
IF Save is activated while the active tab is not dirty, THEN the RequestBar shall perform no write and no markClean.
WHEN the user presses Cmd-Enter, the RequestBar shall route to the same Send path as the Send button under the same empty-url guard, acting on the active tab regardless of focus.
WHEN the user presses Cmd-S, the RequestBar shall route to the Save path and shall call preventDefault so the native save action does not fire.
WHEN the user changes the method, the RequestBar shall not modify the URL buffer or its caret position.
WHILE Share is rendered, the RequestBar shall present it token-styled in its final action slot wired to a disabled no-op stub.
WHILE a URL exceeds the input width, the RequestBar shall scroll it horizontally within the field without reflowing the method pill or Send.
WHEN the application shell renders, the App shall mount the RequestBar into the Shell request-pane slot.
The RequestBar shall source its method list and HttpMethod type from the lib httpMethods module, which requestSpec also references as its method type.
The exported RequestBar props, the updateActiveSpec action, and the METHODS constant and HttpMethod type shall carry documentation comments.
The constitution section 4 sole-subscriber rule shall be updated to record RequestBar as the tabsStore spec-edit subscriber.
The renderer source shall pass strict type-checking.
The codebase shall pass ESLint without new errors.
The project shall build cleanly.
The new RequestBar and lib source shall contain no inline style attributes.
The new renderer modules shall not import the electron or node built-in modules directly.
The RequestBar and tabsStore test suites shall pass.

ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI pipeline changes; tests run via existing npm scripts (vitest/playwright), not a new pipeline stage.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No new git hooks or commit gates; the project's existing forge gates apply unchanged.

constraints.content: Renderer tier organization: RequestBar is a flat organism (downward-only imports, no sibling-organism import, @renderer alias, no barrel files); the organisms/<domain>/ subfolder is created only at >=2 single-domain components.
requestSpec stays a pure data module — imported by tabsStore, never by components; RequestBar must reach the method list/type via lib/httpMethods, not via a requestSpec import.
Gate behavior with Vitest + Testing Library (jsdom) and Playwright CT for focus/keyboard fidelity, co-located under __tests__/ split .test.tsx/.ct.tsx.
Renderer modules import no Node/Electron APIs and resolve cross-module code via the @renderer alias.
Mutate tabsStore state only through store actions; RequestBar writes method+url exclusively via the new updateActiveSpec action and becomes the spec-edit subscriber of tabsStore alongside TabBar.
Reuse the existing Dropdown molecule, Icon atom, cx(), and tokens.css; introduce no new runtime dependency and no second overlay library.

design/reference.html is a look/behavior reference only — never reproduce its markup or generated cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel); rebuild with semantic class names from each element's role.

Verify RequestBar visual fidelity via tiered checks — computed-style exact equality on enumerated props plus a thresholded screenshot diff in Playwright CT — per the 005 precedent; CT fixtures must import tokens.css, set data-mstyle on the host, and apply the Radix two-step dismiss gate.
The existing TabBar selection/close/new-blank behavior and the tabsStore lifecycle actions must remain unchanged; updateActiveSpec is additive.

affected_areas.impact: Create new — flat organism: method Dropdown trigger + URL input + Send/Save/Share; per-field tabsStore selectors; ⌘↵/⌘S document-keydown handlers; onSend prop; token-bound semantic classes, zero inline styles
Create new — export const METHODS (7 methods, display order) + export type HttpMethod = typeof METHODS[number]; lib-only, no component/store imports
Modify — re-point method from string to HttpMethod imported from httpMethods.ts (single source); makeBlankRequest GET seed stays valid
Modify — add updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>) action: shallow-merge into active tab spec, set dirty=true only on actual change, resolve active tab internally; existing actions unchanged
Modify — add panes={{ request: <RequestBar onSend={...} /> }} to the existing <Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} /> element; ToastProvider/Shell wiring otherwise unchanged
Modify — update §4 'TabBar.tsx is the sole subscriber' wording to admit RequestBar as the spec-edit subscriber of tabsStore (documentation-only; no behavior change to TabBar)

Create new / extend — RequestBar unit (trim guard, method-switch independence, Save dirty/no-op) + CT (layout fidelity, disabled Send, ⌘↵/⌘S, method-dropdown dismiss two-step gate, per-tab isolation); tabsStore.test.ts gains updateActiveSpec cases (dirty-on-change, no-op no-flip, per-tab isolation)

out_of_scope.content: HTTP execution and the response view — Send fires an in-renderer onSend intent only; the engine and response rendering are separate tasks.
{{variable}} token highlighting, unknown-var flagging, and template interpolation — the URL is a plain bound string; this belongs to epic D (environment-model / variable-interpolation).
Request-editing panels (params / headers / body / auth editors) and request sub-tabs — RequestBar edits only method+url; auth is untouched.
Durable persistence to disk / collections — Save acts on the in-memory spec via markClean only; the persistence port is its own task.
Real Share behavior (copy-as-cURL / link / export) and URL parsing / validation / autocomplete / history — Share is a disabled no-op stub and the URL is a plain string.
Creating organisms/request/ or relocating TabBar — placement stays flat this task; the domain folder is created later when a 2nd request-domain organism crosses the >=2 trigger.

open_questions.content: The method-pill trigger's accessible-name handling (mirror 005's aria approach so the method is announced once, not doubled) is a /plan detail; the exact design-reference geometry (heights/padding) is pulled from design/reference.html at /plan.

risks.risk: updateActiveSpec over-dirties: flipping dirty on a no-op patch (same value) would break the non-dirty Save no-op contract.
risks.mitigation: Set dirty only when a value actually changes; unit-test both no-op-no-flip and change-flips, plus per-tab isolation, before wiring the UI.

risks.risk: Narrowing requestSpec.method string->HttpMethod surfaces a hidden non-listed method assignment elsewhere, breaking the build.
risks.mitigation: CBM-confirmed the only writer is makeBlankRequest seeding GET; the typecheck/build gate catches any stray assignment; lift the type into httpMethods and re-point in one change.

risks.risk: CT flakiness: the Radix dismiss arm-race and a missing data-mstyle/tokens context produce intermittent false pass/fail on the method-dropdown and fidelity tests.
risks.mitigation: Apply the two-step dismiss gate (await animations + yield setTimeout(0)); fixtures import tokens.css and set data-mstyle on the host per the 005 CT lesson.

risks.risk: Mount-point drift: docs/architecture.md shows a stale propless <Shell/> while App.tsx actually passes tabs={<TabBar/>}, risking a wrong wiring assumption.
risks.mitigation: Spec §2 uses the verified App.tsx wiring (add panes onto the existing <Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} /> element); refresh the architecture doc at /finalize.

risks.risk: RequestBar as a 2nd tabsStore subscriber over-renders if it subscribes to the whole tabs array instead of the active fields.
risks.mitigation: Use per-field selectors for the active tab's method+url (mirror TabBar's memoized selector pattern).
